title: Quantitative Photoacoustic Tomography (qPAT)
sub_domain: Quantitative multi-spectral chromophore recovery (multi-physics joint inverse)
source_data_quality_note: v3 standalone multi-physics genesis Principle. Joint forward operator couples three physics regimes: (1) optical radiative transfer / diffusion (light fluence in scattering tissue), (2) thermoelastic source generation (Grueneisen-coupled energy absorption -> initial pressure), (3) acoustic wave propagation (initial pressure -> detected ultrasound). Recovers tissue optical absorption coefficient mu_a(r, lambda) at multiple wavelengths from measured pressure waveforms. Distinct from L1-041 PAT (acoustic-only with absorption as input) by primitive set, solution_space (3D-spectral chromophore vs 3D pressure), and forward operator scope. Authored under the standalone-as-default v3 design (see pwm-team/pwm_product/genesis/PWM_V3_STANDALONE_VS_COMPOSITE.md).
related_principles: L1-040 (DOT — diffuse optical tomography component, informational)

E.description: Quantitative Photoacoustic Tomography (qPAT): joint multi-physics forward couples optical radiative transfer / diffusion (light fluence Phi(r, lambda) under tissue scattering and absorption), Grueneisen-coupled thermoelastic source generation (initial pressure p_0 = Gamma * mu_a * Phi), and acoustic wave propagation (p(r, t) from p_0(r) under tissue speed-of-sound c(r)). The forward DAG has 8 primitives with two coupling constraints (n_c = 2): (i) light-fluence to energy-absorption multiplicative coupling H = mu_a * Phi, position-dependent and saturating at high absorption; (ii) thermoelastic-source to acoustic-wave causal coupling p_0 -> p, governed by the homogeneous wave equation in tissue. Recovery is posed as the joint inverse problem that recovers mu_a(r, lambda) (and optionally mu_s'(r) via multi-illumination) from measured p(r, t) across N_wavelengths. Multi-spectral acquisition disambiguates chromophore concentrations (oxy-hemoglobin, deoxy-hemoglobin, melanin, lipid, water, contrast agents). Difficulty tier delta = 5 with raw condition number kappa ~ 300 (limited by acoustic bandwidth and high-absorption non-uniqueness) and effective kappa_eff ~ 40 after model-based two-step or one-step Bal-Uhlmann reconstruction. Mismatch parameters: mu_s_prime_uncertainty, gruneisen_uncertainty, acoustic_speed_heterogeneity, transducer_position_error, light_fluence_inhomogeneity, background_absorption_drift. Additive Gaussian thermal noise sets the data-fidelity floor. See forward_model field for the closed-form joint imaging equation.
E.forward_model: Phi(r, lambda) = integral G_optical(r, r'; mu_a, mu_s', lambda) * S(r', lambda) dr'; H(r, lambda) = mu_a(r, lambda) * Phi(r, lambda); p_0(r) = Gamma * sum_lambda H(r, lambda); p(r_d, t) = integral G_acoustic(r_d, r'; c) * p_0(r') dr'; jointly invert p(r_d, t) -> mu_a(r, lambda) given mu_s', Gamma, c, illumination S
E.world_state_x: 3D multi-spectral tissue optical absorption coefficient mu_a(r, lambda) [1/cm], from which chromophore concentrations (HbO2, Hb, water, lipid, melanin, contrast agents) are decomposed via spectral linear unmixing. Range: 0.05-50 1/cm depending on chromophore.
E.observation_y: Multi-wavelength time-resolved pressure waveforms p(r_d, t, lambda) at transducer positions r_d; multi-spectral illumination S(r, lambda) is calibrated input.

G.dag: L.optical_source -> L.optical_diffusion -> L.beer_lambert -> L.thermoelastic_grueneisen -> L.acoustic_wave_propagation -> L.transducer_detection -> int.temporal -> int.spectral
coupling_constraints: fluence_to_absorption: H = mu_a * Phi — multiplicative coupling between optical fluence and absorption coefficient; position-dependent; saturating at high absorption (the canonical qPAT non-uniqueness in single-illumination two-step inversion)

thermoelastic_to_acoustic: p_0 = Gamma * H -> p(r,t) via homogeneous wave equation — causal time-domain coupling; Grueneisen Gamma varies with tissue type and temperature (~0.2 in water, ~0.7 in lipid)

W.regime: Existence of recovered 3D multi-spectral absorption coefficient mu_a(r, lambda) is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds under multi-illumination or multi-wavelength acquisition (Bal-Uhlmann 2010; Bal-Ren 2011); single-illumination single-wavelength qPAT is non-unique due to the multiplicative H = mu_a * Phi coupling and is excluded from the spec range. Stability is moderately conditioned (kappa_eff ~ 40 after model-based reconstruction) — acoustic bandwidth dominates spatial resolution; mu_s_prime_uncertainty dominates absorption-coefficient bias; gruneisen_uncertainty contributes a scaling factor. Joint Hadamard well-posedness for the coupled optical-thermoelastic-acoustic forward is established by Cox-Arridge-Beard (2009), Bal-Uhlmann (2010), Bal-Ren (2011), Tarvainen et al. (2013), and Cox-Tarvainen-Arridge (2014). See joint_well_posedness_references.

C.solver_class: linear-operator + convex optimisation [two-step: UBP + GN-IR for mu_a; one-step: Bal-Uhlmann joint inversion] | gradient-based MCMC [Bayesian qPAT, Tarvainen]| linear-operator + deep neural prior [DAS-Net, J-Net]
C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: ||mu_a_hat - mu_a||_2 <= C1 * sigma_noise / sqrt(N_wavelengths * N_transducers * N_time_samples). Multiplicative-coupling-induced bias adds + C2 * |mu_s_prime_uncertainty| + C3 * |gruneisen_uncertainty| + C4 * |acoustic_speed_heterogeneity| + C5 * ||mu_a - mu_a_s||_1 (best s-sparse / piecewise-smooth approximation residual). Two-step inversion: q=1 (linear) for acoustic step, q=2 (quadratic) for optical step under L1-edge prior. One-step Bal-Uhlmann inversion: q=2 quadratic under multi-illumination data. Deep-prior variants achieve faster initial convergence with bounded systematic bias. epsilon_fn captures monotone quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(H * W * Z * N_wavelengths * (transducer_count * time_samples)^(2/3)) per iteration via k-Wave time-domain forward / adjoint; frequency-domain variants O(H W Z N_wavelengths log(H W Z)); learned variants O(H W Z N_wavelengths * F_theta_cost) per forward pass

joint_well_posedness_references: Cox BT, Arridge SR, Beard PC (2009) Quantitative spectroscopic photoacoustic imaging: A review. J Biomed Opt 17(6):061202.
Bal G, Uhlmann G (2010) Inverse diffusion theory of photoacoustics. Inverse Problems 26(8):085010.
Bal G, Ren K (2011) Multi-source quantitative photoacoustic tomography in a diffusive regime. Inverse Problems 27(7):075003.
Tarvainen T, Pulkkinen A, Cox BT, Kaipio JP, Arridge SR (2013) Bayesian image reconstruction in quantitative photoacoustic tomography. IEEE Trans Med Imaging 32(12):2287-2298.
Cox BT, Tarvainen T, Arridge SR (2014) Multiple illumination quantitative photoacoustic tomography using transport and diffusion models. Contemp Math 615:1-12.
Buchmann J, Guggenheim JA, Zhang E, Beard PC, Cox BT (2017) Quantitative PA tomography of deep tissue in the limit of weak scattering. Phys Med Biol 62(20):8295-8311.
Yao J, Wang LV (2018) Recent progress in photoacoustic molecular imaging. Curr Opin Chem Biol 45:104-112.
Hauptmann A, Cox BT (2020) Deep learning in photoacoustic tomography: current approaches and future directions. J Biomed Opt 25(11):112903.

distinctness_audit.rationale: L1-041 PAT uses sensing_mechanism = photoacoustic_pa with primitives focused on the acoustic forward (treats absorption A(r) as input) and solution_space = 3D pressure / activity. L1-504 qPAT uses sensing_mechanism = thermoelastic_with_optical_diffusion with primitives spanning optical (L.optical_diffusion, L.beer_lambert), thermoelastic (L.thermoelastic_grueneisen), and acoustic (L.acoustic_wave_propagation, L.transducer_detection); solution_space = 3D_spectral_chromophore_quantitative (recovers mu_a(r, lambda) and chromophore concentrations). Jaccard distance over (sensing_mechanism, solution_space, primitives) ~ 0.42 — solidly Distinct under v1 advisory thresholds (>= 0.30). Adds three new primitives (L.optical_diffusion, L.beer_lambert, L.thermoelastic_grueneisen) and the multiplicative non-linear coupling H = mu_a * Phi that is absent from L1-041's linear acoustic forward.

v3_metadata.clinical_context: Active research / early clinical: multi-spectral qPAT recovers oxygen saturation maps (sO2), total hemoglobin, lipid/water fraction, and exogenous contrast-agent uptake. Applications in oncology (tumor angiogenesis, hypoxia mapping, treatment response), vascular imaging (peripheral artery disease, stroke), lymph-node mapping (breast cancer staging), preclinical small-animal imaging. UTSW imaging research has active photoacoustic programs. Commercial systems: iThera Medical MSOT inSight/Acuity, FUJIFILM-VisualSonics Vevo LAZR. Imaging depth typically 1-5 cm depending on wavelength.
